goal.objective: produce a discussion-ready research idea brainstorm memo rather than a project execution spec

audience: Primary use: discussion, prioritization, and next-round reading / deeper thinking

in_scope: tensions, missing pieces, and academically meaningful axes suggested by the literature
out_of_scope: ideas that only work as benchmark-wrapper restatements

constraints: Evidence mode: abstract by default unless fulltext is explicitly requested
Discussion horizon: directions should be strong enough to discuss, not necessarily ready to execute immediately
Preference: fewer, sharper directions over a large templated pool
Memo contract: each lead direction must expose a distinct thesis line, an explicit rank basis, a quick kill criterion, and paper-specific reading notes

rubric.name: discussion_worthiness
rubric.note: is this direction worth a serious PI/PhD discussion right now?

rubric.name: academic_value
rubric.note: could it open a meaningful paper/thesis-worthy research angle?

rubric.name: evidence_grounding
rubric.note: can we point to concrete literature tensions rather than pure speculation?

rubric.name: direction_distinctness
rubric.note: is it genuinely different from neighboring directions, not just a wording variant?

rubric.name: first_probe_clarity
rubric.note: is there a plausible low-cost first probe that would teach us something?

rubric.name: thesis_potential
rubric.note: does it plausibly grow beyond a one-off note into a stronger line of work?

table_policy: Use tables for signals, screening, and shortlist convergence.
Reserve prose for the final brainstorm memo and appendix only.
The appendix must function as a reading guide, not as a generic reminder list.
open_questions: None by default; refine interactively when the user provides extra constraints.
